title: rsac is the ground-truth capture API; downstreams adapt to it
rationale: rsac is a cross-platform audio CAPTURE library (system/device/app/process-tree; WASAPI/PipeWire/CoreAudio; rtrb SPSC bridge). GOAL: design the BEST-possible, most performant + feature-rich capture API (helpers, macros, types) — rsac DEFINES the UX that downstreams consume; it is NOT retrofitted to any one consumer. audio-graph (../audio-graph) is one EXAMPLE consumer whose pain points are EVIDENCE of missing affordances, not a spec. Scope stays capture-only per VISION.md (no DSP/mixing/encoding). RT callback thread NEVER alloc/lock/block. Module DAG core->bridge->audio->api. Verify tiers: Windows-native, WSL (feat_linux), Go (rsac-ffi staticlib), macOS via GH Actions CI. Commits: no Co-Authored-By/byline. Every non-fixed review comment -> tracking issue.

name: verification toolchain + os-eco coordination
description: How to verify changes across platforms and coordinate workflows via seeds/mulch

name: goal backlog: 56 seeds tasks across 6 waves
description: The decomposed executable backlog for the rsac goal, loaded into seeds

name: binding crates must select rsac backend per-target
description: rsac gates platform backends behind feat_windows/feat_linux/feat_macos; enabling the wrong one on a host does not compile

title: Bridge counters + producing-state on CapturingStream trait
rationale: Prerequisite for stream_stats() and windowed backpressure; exposes counters BridgeShared already maintains through the public trait with default-0 bodies so all existing impls/mocks keep compiling.

title: AudioBuffer metering added in core/buffer.rs; call-site refactor deferred to file owners
rationale: STRICT area boundary: this wave-1 agent owns only src/core/buffer.rs, so it cannot touch src/main.rs / src/bin/pipewire_test.rs / rsac-python even though rsac-9a18 acceptance criteria require deleting their local meters.

title: CaptureTarget canonical string grammar for FromStr/TryFrom/Display round-trip
rationale: Downstream waves (bindings, CLI arg parsing) need a single canonical, lossless string form for CaptureTarget. Chose a scheme:body grammar with first-colon-only split so device ids with colons survive, and AudioError::InvalidParameter to match existing api.rs error style.

name: Public read-path types must not depend on cfg-gated imports
description: api.rs gated AudioFormat import to Linux warning-cleanliness, but format() needs it everywhere

name: bridge pop() allocates user copy by design; alloc-free guarantee is producer-only
description: BridgeConsumer::pop does original.clone() (ring_buffer.rs:463) allocating a fresh user Vec every call; this is intentional (non-RT consumer thread). ADR-0001 alloc-free guarantee covers ONLY BridgeProducer::push_samples_or_drop. Any alloc-counting test/bench MUST exclude pop() from the measured region or it will see one alloc per pop.

name: Wave-1 gotchas: doc-link + global-allocator test parallelism
description: Two CI-breaking patterns Wave-1 review caught; avoid in later waves

title: UserFacingError carries both kind and backend_code
rationale: Orchestrator brief and seed spec disagreed on the field set; superset of both is non-breaking thanks to #[non_exhaustive], satisfies all acceptance criteria, and gives UIs both the high-level category and the raw OS code.

name: wasapi 0.23 get_device replaces hand-rolled collection scan

title: AudioDevice::kind() added as fallible provided trait method
rationale: Additive/non-breaking: provided method so external impls keep compiling and inherit PlatformNotSupported until they override. Fallible (AudioResult<DeviceKind>) resolves M1 api-fit clash and lets CoreAudio return Err when scope is indeterminate. Windows delegates to existing IMMEndpoint::GetDataFlow probe.

name: napi-Float32Array-zero-widen
description: NAPI AudioChunk delivers f32 via Float32Array, not Vec<f64>

name: py-to_bytes-le-no-30
description: Python to_bytes() uses to_le_bytes loop, no from_raw_parts (#30)

title: BackpressureReport canonical-defined in introspection.rs; lib.rs re-export + windowed wiring are cross-area follow-ups
rationale: stats-observability owns ONLY introspection.rs + api.rs; the lib.rs re-export and the CapturingStream/BridgeStream windowed override live in files owned by other areas, so this area shipped the type + a read-side lifetime fallback and recorded the wiring as follow-ups.

title: Order C-layer callback clear before cgo.Handle.Delete to fix #28 Go UAF
rationale: cgo.Handle.Value() panics if the handle was Deleted; a callback in-flight on the FFI delivery thread during Close() could resolve a just-deleted handle. Tearing down the C delivery path (set_callback NULL + capture_free) before Delete, plus a top-level recover in goAudioCallback, converts the residual race from a process-aborting UAF into a harmlessly dropped delivery.

name: binding-feature-preset-and-version-lockstep
description: ci-and-presets area: canonical per-target backend selection convention (docs/features.md) + versioning/ABI lockstep contract (RELEASE_PROCESS.md/CHANGELOG/ci.yml)

name: bridge pop now moves ring buffer; recycles a pooled spare (no clone/memcpy)
description: rsac-17d1 changed BridgeConsumer::pop: it MOVES the ring's AudioBuffer to the user (no original.clone()) and recycles a SPARE Vec (from a consumer-side spare_pool, or freshly allocated off-RT when the pool drains) back to the producer free-list. Net: per-pop full-buffer memcpy eliminated; consumer alloc count is equal-or-better (alloc, no copy). Supersedes the framing in mx-c81d32: pop no longer clones, but may still allocate one spare Vec per pop once the seeded spare_pool empties (off-RT, by design). ADR-0001 producer alloc-free guarantee unchanged. Any alloc-probe must still exclude pop().

name: bridge-zerocopy SampleRing: f32 ring + parallel metadata sidecar, all-or-nothing chunk
description: rsac-3616 added feature bridge-zerocopy (default OFF) + create_sample_ring/SampleRingProducer/SampleRingConsumer in ring_buffer.rs. Sample ring is rtrb::RingBuffer<f32> written via write_chunk_uninit + CopyToUninit (copy_to_uninit on the two wrapped MaybeUninit slices) + unsafe commit_all — zero Vec/AudioBuffer alloc on producer. A parallel rtrb::RingBuffer<ChunkMeta> sidecar carries (len,channels,sample_rate,timestamp_nanos; u64::MAX = no-ts). Producer checks BOTH rings have room before committing so a chunk is atomic (consumer never sees partial). Consumer reads meta, read_chunk(len).as_slices() -> extend_from_slice -> commit_all, reconstructs AudioBuffer (user Vec alloc on consumer thread). rtrb bumped 0.3.3->0.3.4 (purely additive).

title: Windowed drop-rate atomics live in BridgeShared; BackpressureReport type + api wiring is the stats area's job
rationale: bridge-internals owns only ring_buffer.rs/stream.rs; the BackpressureReport type lives in core/introspection.rs and the trait/AudioCapture wiring in api.rs, both owned by the stats-area agent. So bridge-internals exposes the RT atomics + read API and stops there to avoid editing unowned files.

title: push_samples_guarded (catch_unwind) provided as producer method; FFI call-site wiring deferred to platform agents
rationale: rsac-d0ba acceptance allows 'the producer method itself' to be the guarded path. The macos/windows/linux thread.rs call sites are owned by platform agents, so bridge-internals provides+tests the guarded method and leaves the call-site swap as a follow-up rather than editing unowned files.

name: Wave-2 gotchas: MSRV vs is_multiple_of, doc-link classes, Rc-move-in-closure
description: Recurring CI-breakers from Wave 2 for later waves to pre-empt
